title: Native Windows consecutive profile-switch: deterministic run_post + autonomous harness
description: PAB detour now owns 0x7ad1c0 and calls system_quit_menu_window_run_post (fixes ghosting + non-interactive ProfileSelect install race). Build autonomous harness to load 2nd/3rd character consecutively from the 169-Invades multi-char save, quit via Return-to-Desktop each iteration. Prove via RAM oracles (distinct char identity per switch, 0 softlock, 0 msgbox).
notes: HARNESS FEASIBILITY (empirical, 2026-07-15): built switch_harness_discovery_tick (lifecycle.rs, marker-gated ER_EFFECTS_SWITCH_HARNESS_DISCOVERY) that blocks the keyboard + pulses DIK_ESCAPE in-world and logs every MenuWindowJob::Run filename via run_post. RESULT: after ESC, only HUD/boot filenames appeared (01_000_FE, 01_100_Clock, 01_080_EmergencyNotice, 01_090_SummonMessage, 01_060_Caption, 05_000_Title, 01_900_Black, 02_903_NowLoading2) -- NO 02_000_IngameTop, NO menu opened. So the DInput keyboard block/inject vehicle (the Proton path in input_blocker.rs) does NOT reach the game's menu input on NATIVE WINDOWS (game likely uses raw input / a different device). HARNESS BLOCKER: need a native-Windows input vehicle -- verify with a movement-key (DIK_W) test whether DInput reaches the game AT ALL, else use PostMessageW(WM_KEYDOWN) (hooks.rs:285) or a native menu-open call. Also still need the user's actual menu-open keybind. Discovery code is env/marker-gated OFF for product; harness driver NOT built beyond discovery.

title: Deep-RE the native-Windows portrait render-drive crash (FUN_141e90290 rcx=0x20) for a safe animated drive
description: User chose No-Compromises path (2026-07-15): reverse-engineer a genuinely safe animated portrait drive on native Windows. The drive crashes at FUN_141e90290 rcx=0x20 -- a half-seeded GX offscreen resource (null wrapper+0x40) dereferenced by the GX resource-state classifier while the engine's OWN ResMan offscreen render runs mid-build (+0x754/+0x755 in flight). Existing guard profile_offscreen_gx_resources_ready (lookat_bone_hooks.rs:286) checks wrapper+inner readiness but was insufficient (native still crashed) so the whole drive was gated off. Goal: Ghidra-decompile FUN_141e90290/FUN_141a02de0/FUN_141a853a0 + the engine offscreen build lifecycle, find the serialization primitive that lets us drive ONLY when the engine is provably not rendering, gate strictly on it, native-validate. Refs er-effects-rs-n4x.

title: Native-Windows save picker: render list on overlay + keyboard input
description: On native Windows the loading experience is owned by the separate-window overlay (own D3D12 device). When no autoload save is configured the game shows a save picker; we must render the save-file list ON the overlay and wire keyboard navigation/pick. The existing WH_KEYBOARD_LL path (ensure_save_picker_keyboard_hook / save_picker_overlay_input_tick) was designed for the Wine Present-hook thread, which never installs on native Windows. Need the overlay's own message-pump thread to source keyboard input for the picker. User will test interactively (launch without a configured save, drive with keyboard).
close_reason: Proven live end-to-end on native Windows (no-save boot): picker armed + boot held; WH_KEYBOARD_LL hook drove full keyboard nav (src_hook=true) across AppData->C:\->drives D/M/O/P/T/V/X/Y-> A:\...\169-Invades\ER0000.sl2; file pick->character sub-picker->slot 0->direct-file stage + SHGetFolderPathW/CreateFileW redirect->save-check hold released->character loaded in-world; clean exit no AV. Commit 6fa84c9.

title: Native-Windows loading overlay: separate window + own D3D12 device
description: ARCHITECTURE PIVOT (user 2026-07-15, 'own what the user sees across the loading experience'). Root cause established over 17 native-Windows runs (er-effects-rs-n4x): compositing on the GAME's shared D3D12 device crashes the strict native AMD driver at every phase (shared-device contention with the game's async rendering). vkd3d/Proton isolates it; native Windows does not. FIX: don't composite on the game's device at all. Instead OWN the screen with a SEPARATE always-on-top borderless overlay window + our OWN D3D12 device + swapchain, covering the game. Render the full loading experience (loading bar, static portrait, save picker) on our independent device -- no shared-device race, cannot crash the game. Game boots+loads underneath headless-to-screen; hand off (hide/destroy our window) when player_present (world loaded). Input: reuse the WH_KEYBOARD_LL low-level hook (focus-independent) for the picker. Requires BORDERLESS mode (overlay windows can't cover exclusive fullscreen). Reuse: HDR pack helper (pack_rgba8_to_r10g10b10a2), the CPU bar/text rasterizer, the save-picker model. Retire on native Windows: the game-swapchain composite path (kept for Wine). Plan: (1) minimal proof -- window+device+swapchain, clear-to-color over the game, hide on world load, verify no crash + visible; (2) add bar; (3) add picker+input; (4) add static portrait; (5) handoff polish.
notes: PROGRESS 2026-07-15: overlay working on native Windows. Validated: (1) separate window + own D3D12 device/swapchain, stable 60s, reaches gameplay (run18, stage=10, 3662 frames); (2) ownership cycle -- SHOW while player absent, HIDE in gameplay (run19, show=0 in-world); (3) loading BAR driven by real progress (run20, USER CONFIRMED visible); (4) caption TEXT via CPU-rasterize(boot_draw_text_rgb, exposed pub(crate)) + upload buffer + CopyTextureRegion on OUR device (run21). Render path: ClearRTV bg+bar rects, then RT->COPY_DEST, upload+copy a text strip, ->PRESENT. Swapchain is 8-bit R8G8B8A8 (no HDR pack needed on our own swapchain). Progress source: LOADING_SCREEN_BAR_PROGRESS_PERMILLE (native loading bar) + boot creep. Commits on branch fix/windows-gx-resource-null-crash. NEXT: (a) save PICKER -- render the file list (reuse save_picker_overlay model + the text-region blit) + wire WH_KEYBOARD_LL nav into it; the picker shows during the boot cover when no save provided (user wants to test w/ keyboard); (b) static portrait + stats. The upload/CopyTextureRegion text path is the reusable primitive for both.

title: y22i: RE-grounded FUN_141e90290 crash fix (needs Ghidra/deobf host)
description: The native-Windows loading-screen AV FUN_141e90290 (deobf 0x141e90290, rcx=0x20 reads [rcx+0x10]->+0x30) is a game-side advance-before-seed race in the CSMenuProfModelRend offscreen GX pipeline: our +0x754/+0x755 kick drives the engine to build the profile model+offscreen mid-load, and the engine's own ResMan-scheduled offscreen render reaches the GX resource-state classifier with a half-seeded resource. Session 2026-07-15 shipped a MITIGATION (suppress our Present composite while +0x754/+0x755 build latches are set, present_overlay.rs profile_model_build_in_flight) because the composite's concurrent GPU device work was the timing trigger vs 6 clean runs. Two deeper fixes need the Ghidra dump + eldenring-deobf.bin (both live under /home/banon, ABSENT on the /home/choza validation host): (1) correct profile_offscreen_gx_resources_ready in lookat_bone_hooks.rs:286-302 -- it misreads off+0x58 (a scene-registered BYTE flag per portrait_worker.rs:449-450) as a 4th wrapper pointer, so it perma-returns false on ALL platforms (block_off_resource fired but never let the drive through); re-RE the true off+0x40..+0x58 layout and the resource-state field. (2) Add a null-check DETOUR on FUN_141e90290 mirroring the proven Scaleform descriptor guard (scaleform_descriptor_guard.rs) -- needs the function's return contract + prologue from the dump. Do this work on the RE host (banon) with scripts/ghidra-query.sh + scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py.
notes: DECISIVE ISOLATION 2026-07-15 (run14, drive-off-on-native + composite gate). Result: 60s cap, ZERO crashes, player_seen=true, char='Experiment Bean' -- user confirmed the game reaches the loading screen + in-game and 'that all works'. CONCLUSION (proven, not inferred): the character-profile RENDER-DRIVE (force_profile_render kick + portrait pump) is THE native-Windows crash cause; it makes the game rasterize its own profile offscreen mid-load which the strict AMD D3D12 driver faults on (every crash site chased -- 0x1e90290/0x1e862fd/0xd6510d -- was downstream of this drive). vkd3d/Proton tolerates it => Linux fine. The DISPLAY/composite path is NOT the cause. Committed c9a9e5a: is_native_windows() gates force_profile_render_enabled + portrait_render_drive_enabled OFF on native Windows (env ER_EFFECTS_ALLOW_NATIVE_PROFILE_DRIVE=1 override); composite early-boot gate. DLL now STABLE on native Windows. CONSEQUENCE: the ANIMATED portrait cannot work on native Windows (needs the drive) -- architectural. REMAINING OPPORTUNITY: the loading BAR + a STATIC portrait do NOT need the drive; wire the composite to a DRIVE-INDEPENDENT display gate (game's own CS::LoadingScreen active / oracle_now_loading, not PROFILE_LOADSCREEN_TABLE_BUILDS which only fires with the drive) and test if they display visible pixels safely. Ghidra dump at /home/choza/ghidra_y22i if needed.

title: y22i Windows validation: A/B guard vs control for 0xec95d1 Scaleform crash
description: Execute docs/y22i-windows-validation-handoff.md on the native Windows dual-boot (real RX 6900 XT). Build guarded DLL (59b025b) and control DLL (de53e76) natively with MSVC, run me3 offline A/B probes, confirm control reproduces access-violation rva=0xec95d1 and guard build shows oracle_scaleform_desc_guard_installed=1, oracle_scaleform_desc_provider_null_hits>0, no AV. Record evidence + bd memory.
notes: DECISIVE FINDINGS 2026-07-15 (native Windows RX6900XT + Special K + HDR). All committed on branch fix/windows-gx-resource-null-crash. FIXED+PROVEN: (1) swapchain discovery (present_hook_hits 0->196/41; QI-fallback accepts SpecialK-wrapped swapchain, vtable in SpecialK64.dll, accept_path=2). (2) HDR composite (backbuffer=R10G10B10A2 fmt24; pack_rgba8_to_r10g10b10a2 + BackbufferEncoding; boot_view_draw_hits 0->3). (3) device-removed crash 0x1e8ad57 (boot-pump self-present used a wrong-state PRESENT<->COPY_DEST barrier at arbitrary time -> hr=0x887a0005; FIX: on wrapped swapchain accept_path==2, skip self-present, hand off to Present detour which draws only inside Present when backbuffer is guaranteed PRESENT state). (4) GX classifier consumer guard on FUN_141e90290 (RVA 0x1e90290, NOT 0xe90290 - watch the digit): null_hits=71, that crash GONE. KEY DIAGNOSTIC: Present hook installed + composite DISABLED = 60s CLEAN (no crash); composite drawing = crash. So our concurrent GPU composite submit races the game's own profile-offscreen render (advance-before-seed), producing a null GX resource (rcx=0x20) dispatched to MULTIPLE consumers. REMAINING CRASH (needs Ghidra/deobf host, absent on /home/choza): a SECOND consumer at deobf 0x141e862fd (fault +0x38, rcx=0x20), reached via a DIFFERENT chain than FUN_141a853a0->FUN_141a02de0->0x141e90290. 0x1e862fd crash stack (deobf): 0x141e862fd<-0x141e87eac<-0x141e86db3<-0x141e97b2a<-0x141e883b7 (all 0x141e8/e9 GX cluster). My FUN_141a853a0 @ 0x1a853a0 source-guard did NOT fire (skip_hits=0) = wrong chokepoint; REMOVED it. NEXT (RE host): decompile the 0x141e8xxxx GX cluster to find the true dispatch chokepoint that loads wrapper+0x40 and passes to BOTH 0x141e90290 and 0x141e862fd; guard THAT (or all consumer entries). The consumer-guard PATTERN is proven. Alt lead: test WITHOUT Special K (reduces GPU contention + swapchain wrapping).

title: Overlay can't render: hudhook 0.9.1 DX12 command-queue detection fails on ER 1.16.1
description: In-game smoke test (er-offline.exe, DLL auto-injected via dllMods/LazyLoader) showed the DLL injects and runs stably, but the ImGui overlay never appears. chains-debug.log spams every frame: 'hudhook::hooks::dx12: Couldn't find command queue pointer in swap chain struct (512 out of 512 pointers were readable)' followed by 'Initialization context incomplete' and 'Render error: HRESULT(0xFFFFFFFF)'. Reproduced with ALL other mods disabled (only er_effects_rs.dll loaded), so it is not a mod conflict. The pinned hudhook is commit 6a361de (0.9.1); its DX12 hook scans the swapchain struct for the D3D12 command queue pointer and never finds it on this game build/GPU, so the renderer context never completes. Root cause is upstream in hudhook, shared via the fromsoftware-rs 'debug' crate (both it and this DLL depend on the same hudhook git rev, so they must be bumped together).
design: Fix options, in order of preference: (1) Bump hudhook to a version whose DX12 hook obtains the command queue by hooking ID3D12CommandQueue::ExecuteCommandLists instead of scanning the swapchain (later hudhook reworked this); requires coordinating the bump in fromsoftware-rs since the debug crate pins the same rev, and adapting to any ImguiRenderLoop API changes. (2) If staying on 0.9.x, investigate passing the command queue explicitly. (3) Confirm whether a DX11-on-12 path is viable. Note: ProDebug._dll in dllMods is a native (non-hudhook) tool, so it is not a usable comparison.
acceptance_criteria: Overlay renders in-game on ER 1.16.1; chains-debug.log shows a 'Found command queue pointer ... at offset' line and no 'Initialization context incomplete' spam.

Define where recon results live in the repo (e.g. docs/recon or a data file), a stable schema, and a way to consume them so the seeded SpEffect list can be validated and expanded from real data instead of guesswork.

title: Native Windows: CS::MessageBoxDialog built in-world during System>Quit>Load-Profile
description: During the 2026-07-15 live no-save picker run, after the picked character loaded in-world and the user opened System>Quit>Load-Profile (05_010_ProfileSelect), a 01_010_MessageBox / msgbox-builder #2 was built in_world=true right before the clean game exit (debug log +148539ms). AGENTS.md treats every CS::MessageBoxDialog as an investigation trigger. This is NOT at character load (it was ~90s after, during a user-driven quit/profile-switch flow), so likely a quit-confirm or profile-switch save dialog -- but its native side effect must be identified and satisfied/skipped without UI per policy. Not blocking the picker feature (that flow was clean). Reproduce: in-world -> System -> Quit -> Load Profile, watch oracle_msgbox_* and the 01_010_MessageBox AcquireMenuResource.

title: Surface SpEffect apply/remove failures to the user
description: EffectCallKind::apply/remove call player.apply_speffect / remove_speffect and discard the result. If an ID is invalid or the call fails, the overlay shows no feedback. Capture and display success/failure status per call so testers know whether an effect actually applied.
acceptance_criteria: Apply/remove outcomes are surfaced in the overlay (or logged) instead of silently ignored.
close_reason: Per-call [active]/[inactive]/[apply failed] status in overlay, recomputed each tick from the player SpEffect list since the game calls return nothing

title: Harden local-player animation detection
description: current_animation_id() in src/lib.rs reads a single anim_queue slot via (read_idx % len) and treats it as the current animation. If multiple animations are queued or the index semantics differ, the appear-trigger 63010 detection can miss or misfire, causing effects to apply at the wrong time or not at all. Review the time_act anim_queue semantics and make detection robust.
acceptance_criteria: Animation detection reliably identifies the appear trigger; behavior documented and ideally covered by a test or in-game verification note.
close_reason: Animation observation now also scans TimeAct queue slots written since the previous tick (write_idx delta, bounded to one lap), so briefly-played 63010 triggers are not missed; invalid anim ids (<=0) mapped to None; semantics documented

title: Make the seeded SpEffect list data-driven instead of hardcoded constants
description: PLAYER_ALL_BLACK_SPEFFECT_ID and friends are compile-time constants in src/lib.rs with names baked into Default::default. As the recon expands the list this will not scale. Load the named-call list from an embedded/config data file so updating IDs does not require editing Rust source.
acceptance_criteria: Named SpEffect calls are loaded from a data source (embedded file or config) rather than inline constants.
close_reason: Seeded calls now live in data/effects.json, embedded at compile time via er-effects-data; parse errors surface in the overlay instead of panicking

title: Allow adding arbitrary SpEffect IDs at runtime from the overlay
description: The ImGui overlay only toggles three hardcoded NamedEffectCall entries. There is no way to enter an arbitrary SpEffect ID at runtime for testing. Add an input field + 'Add' button so testers can apply/remove any ID without recompiling.

close_reason: Overlay gained a Custom SpEffect ID input + Add button with duplicate-ID guard; added entries support the same apply/remove/status handling

title: Avoid rebuilding the dotnet bridge on every param query
description: ensure_bridge_project rewrites the csproj + Program.cs and 'dotnet run --configuration Release' on every query_param_rows call, recompiling each time. For batch validation this is slow. title: Extend EffectCallKind beyond SpEffect
description: EffectCallKind is an enum with a single SpEffect variant, signaling intent to support other runtime effect calls (e.g. FXR/SFX triggers referenced throughout docs/reference-tree). Generalize the apply/remove dispatch so additional effect kinds can be added without reworking the overlay/state model.

close_reason: EffectCallKind dispatch isolated behind label/apply/remove/is_active + call_kind_from_spec with documented 3-step recipe for new variants; data schema kind field already extensible

value: REAL bc4-PUMP GATE FOUND + OUR-GUARDS-STARVE-IT HYPOTHESIS (2026-07-16, Ghidra + runtime). The switch-load stall root chain (see switch-lock-unified-root-bc4-not-ready-functor): bc4 must pump 1->2->3 for our final functor to fire and set rt5d so the incoming child completes. The pump is done by the quit-save orchestrator FUN_140afb970, which FUN_140aff730 (MoveMapStep update) calls UNCONDITIONALLY every tick (so my old save-gate-diag conclusion 'orchestrator not called' was WRONG -- it IS called). FUN_140afb970 returns WITHOUT pumping unless: (a) BOOL_143d856a0 (force latch) == 0 [else a different path], (b) bVar5 == true, (c) FUN_14067a170 == true. FUN_14067a170 = GameMan->saveState==0 (true on the stall, save_state=0). So the gate is bVar5: bVar5 = ShouldSave() || FUN_140679460() || (GetRequestedSaveSlotLoad() != -1). ShouldSave (0x1406794c0) = GameMan->saveRequested(b72) && !CanShowSaveMenu() && FUN_14080d660()(menu_gate) && bc4!=3. FUN_140679460 (0x140679460) = GameMan+0xb73 && FUN_14080d660() && bc4!=3. GetRequestedSaveSlotLoad = GameMan+0xb78. So bVar5 needs one of: [b72 && !CanShowSaveMenu && menu_gate && bc4!=3] | [b73 && menu_gate && bc4!=3] | [b78 != -1]. On the stall (save-gate-diag showed menu_gate_ok=true, bc4=1!=3, force=0, save_state=0) bVar5 is somehow 0 -> orchestrator bails -> bc4 never pumps. PRIME SUSPECT: OUR OWN GUARDS starve bVar5: (1) the b78 guard (title_tick_cover.rs) FORCES GameMan+0xb78 = -1 (OWN_STEPPER_SLOT_NONE) while world_up -> kills the (b78 != -1) term; (2) if b72/b73 are also 0 (or CanShowSaveMenu()==true kills ShouldSave), bVar5=0. So MY accumulated fixes (b78=-1 guard for the reload-loop, bc4=0 clear) are likely fighting the game's own quit-save pump = the user's 'a world that didn't get torn down fully'. INSTRUMENTATION LANDED (DLL ea129345, save-gate-diag now logs b72/b73/b78/bVar5_est): next stall names the exact failing term. FIX DIRECTION (pending the b72/b73/b78 readout): stop OUR guards from starving the pump during the teardown window -- e.g. do NOT force b78=-1 (or ensure a save condition / let saveRequested drive ShouldSave) so bVar5=true -> orchestrator pumps bc4 1->2->3 -> final functor fires -> rt5d set -> child completes. Do NOT keep the bc4=0 clear (it skips bc4==3). Verify CanShowSaveMenu() too (if true it kills ShouldSave AND clears saveRequested).

UNIFIED ROOT of the switch-load lock (2026-07-16, runtime-proven, DLL reverted to cc33164b). The "2nd bug" (specific re-load stall at MOVE MAP 18/20, rt5d=0) is NOT separate from the original bc4-frozen-at-1 bug -- it is the SAME root. CHAIN (all runtime-confirmed): our final functor (title_tick_cover.rs ~1217, submits the return-title JOB that sets menuData+0x5d=1 and completes teardown) fires ONLY when return_title_job_predicate_bc4 == GAME_MAN_RETURN_TITLE_JOB_PREDICATE_READY (bc4==3) AND phase>=TITLE_OWNER_SEEN AND DIRECT_RETURN_TITLE_CHAIN_SUBMIT_COUNT>0 AND a one-shot. So: bc4 never READY -> final functor never fires -> return-title job never runs -> rt5d never set -> the incoming MoveMapStep child (needs rt5d=1 from the start of STEP_MoveMap to walk to its -1 terminal) parks at 18 -> InGameStep parent (finished == child+0x48==-1) waits forever = the stall. WHY bc4 does not pump on the stuck load: the quit-save orchestrator FUN_140afb970 (pumps bc4 1->2, then FUN_14067aa70 2->3) is called from the OLD world's MoveMapStep step-update FUN_140aff730, which ticks only if the old world's MoveMapStep CSEzUpdateTask is registered/ticking in CSTaskGroup 20. On the accumulating stuck sequence the OLD world does not tear down fully (its MoveMapStep teardown does not run/pump) = the user's "a world that didn't get torn down fully". MY bc4-CLEAR FIX (LOAD-3 FINALIZE FIX) MAKES THIS WORSE on the stuck case: it forces bc4=0 to unblock STEP_MoveMap's per-frame gate (FUN_140679010 clears field_0x4b8 when bc4!=0), but bc4=0 PERMANENTLY SKIPS the bc4==3 the final functor needs. So the clear treats a symptom (the per-frame gate) not the root (functor firing). CORRECT FIX DIRECTION: get bc4 to READY(3) on the stuck load so the final functor fires (build+submit the return-title job / set rt5d) -- either (a) fix why the old world's MoveMapStep teardown does not pump bc4 (task-registration accumulation over N loads), or (b) when bc4 is stuck at 1 with the load stalling, PUMP bc4 1->2->3 via the game's quit-save fns (FUN_14067b840/ba30 then FUN_14067aa70) OR fire the final functor directly, THEN let bc4->0 so STEP_MoveMap's gate clears and the child advances with rt5d set. Do NOT just clear bc4=0 (that is the current inadequate fix). All prior rt5d/menuData+0x5d fix attempts (force-set, defer-clear) FAILED because rt5d was never SET (functor never fired), not cleared. FIX (2026-07-15, native Windows, ER 1.16.2): The System->Quit ghosting (bug 2) AND the 2nd-in-process reload crash (bug 1, garbage-vtable AV at CSGaitemImp::Deserialize 0x67141a) were BOTH caused by a MinHook install REENTRANCY race. install_system_quit_menu_window_job_run_hook (system_quit_ownership_repro.rs:644) and install_system_quit_gaitem_deserialize_hook (system_quit_hooks.rs:531) used a  guard that did NOT block a reentrant call while the first was mid-install (INSTALLED set only on full success). So the install ran twice -> double MhHook::new (ALREADY_CREATED) + double MH_EnableHook -> MH_ERROR_ENABLED -> the handler non-deterministically didn't fire. When the MenuWindowJob::Run hook loses the race: no System-menu hide (ghosting) AND the return-title/continue_confirm reset+feed path never runs so the native deserialize crashes on char#1's stale gaitem table. FIX: replace the guard with an ATOMIC once-claim (compare_exchange NOT_INSTALLED->INSTALLED_YES; reentrant callers bail), accept MH_ERROR_ENABLED as success, roll back to NOT_INSTALLED only on real failure. Also switched from queue_enable+MH_ApplyQueued (shared-queue race) to atomic single-target MH_EnableHook (crate::mh::MH_EnableHook). VERIFIED live: hooked ...(immediate enable, created=true), MenuWindowJob::Run fires reliably (268 ProfileSelect), hide fires, ghosting GONE, and the FIRST profile switch loads cleanly (continue_confirm intercepted -> gaitem pristine-restore released=2572 -> own-load-feed slot=6 ret=1 -> char loads, 0 AVs). ALSO added a backstop reset in the gaitem-deserialize hook (system_quit_hooks.rs) before the native deserialize when SELECTED_SLOT<10. REMAINING (user gate: 'do not mark fixed until we can load more than one character'): the 2ND consecutive switch stalls -- return-title REQUEST+SUBMIT reach the title but continue_confirm never fires the 2nd time (product-core-autoload Continue driver is one-shot; rearm_boot_progress_for_own_menu_load re-arms only the loading BAR, not the Continue driver) -> parked at the covered title = black screen (not a crash). Bugs 2+3 fully fixed+confirmed; bug 1 first-switch works, consecutive pending re-arm. See [[system-quit-menuwindowjob-run-dead-hook-rootcause-2026-07-15]] [[game-is-1162-re-baseline-is-1161-2026-07-15]].

With no controller, XInputGetCapabilities(0)/XInputGetState(0) return ERROR_DEVICE_NOT_CONNECTED (1167), so the game stops polling slot 0 and the fabrication in xinput_get_state_hook never lands. Code already documented this ('XInput is not polled' without a controller: constants/system_quit.rs:44, mod/product_core_own_stepper.rs:1108). FIX (crates/er-effects-rs/src/experiments/input_block.rs): (1) xinput_get_state_hook forces slot 0 to report CONNECTED idle pad (SUCCESS + fresh private XINPUT_KEEPALIVE_PACKET) instead of DEVICE_NOT_CONNECTED when harness armed; (2) NEW xinput_get_capabilities_hook forces slot 0 to enumerate as connected standard gamepad (Type=1 SubType=1), wired into install_xinput_block. Both gated on system_quit_repro_enabled()||inject_nav_enabled() (env/file diagnostic opt-ins) -- never product/default, other slots untouched, zero button synthesis so simulated_button_presses_total unchanged. UNVALIDATED at runtime (live session running; DLL not built). Exercise w/o controller: ER_EFFECTS_SYSTEM_QUIT_REPRO=1 (or marker file), boot, watch debug for 'hooked XInputGetCapabilities' + sq-repro transitions. If still stalls, game may gate on WM_DEVICECHANGE hotplug scan; next lever = DInput-keyboard mirror via InputBlocker::set_injected_key with Xbox->DIK map.

BUG (2026-07-15, native Windows): System->Quit->'Quit Game'/'Return to Desktop' shows a LOADING SCREEN instead of exiting immediately. Intended product behavior: quit == ALT+F4 -- the process terminates at once and the user gets full control of display/mouse/focus right after, no loading-screen/world-teardown visible. Likely our quit path (see 'quit-to-desktop: hooked MenuOffscrRendParam lookup 0x7ff6b9f3ed90' + system-quit-dup Return-to-Desktop routing) lets the game run its world-teardown (which renders a loading screen, possibly under our native overlay) instead of hard ExitProcess/TerminateProcess. FIX direction: on the Return-to-Desktop/Quit action, release input/cursor clip + hard-exit the process immediately (ExitProcess) rather than going through the game's teardown+loadscreen. Third of three System->Quit-menu bugs reported together; see [[native-profile-load-msgbox-crash-1162]].

2ND RUNTIME-ACCUM RE-LOAD LOCK: ROOT PROVEN + FIX APPLIED (2026-07-16, DLL 535d4f93). RUNTIME COMPARISON (same-log, instrumented) proved the sole differentiator between a child that ADVANCES 18->19->20 and the stuck one: menuData+0x5d (return-title byte, rt5d). Advancing children have rt5d 1; the stuck child has rt5d 0 with ALL cVar10 ending-request inputs 0 (end5e 0, force 0, GameMan 0xb7c 0, 0xb7d 0, warpRequested@0x10 0). MECHANISM: InGameStep parent (step7) completes only when the MoveMapStep child reaches its -1 terminal (finished-check FUN_140af5190 = MoveMapStep+0x48 equals -1). STEP_MoveMap walks the child toward -1 only while cVar10 (ending request, written to menuData+0x5e by MoveMapStep::CheckReturnToTitle FUN_140afa7c0) is set. cVar10 inputs: menuData+0x5d, warpRequested, CSSessionManager WaitReload, deadReset==2, force 0x3d856a0, GameMan 0xb7c/0xb7d, pad. OUR OWN continue_confirm (system_quit_repro_guards.rs:1352 clears menuData+0x5d, :1372 clears warp_requested) does this for BOUNCE-PREVENTION (the comment at :1361 names FUN_140afa7c0 as CheckReturnToTitle: warp_requested/+0x5d left set bounces the freshly-loaded RESIDENT world back to title ~4s later). Clearing it stops the bounce but on a slower re-load strands the incoming child at step 18 = the lock. FIX (title_tick_cover.rs SWITCH-ORACLE, ENDING-REQUEST RECOVERY): when the exact stuck signature (ig_d8 1, mms_step 18, end5e 0, rt5d 0, b7c1 1, blocks>0) holds past ENDING_REQUEST_STALL_RELEASE_FRAMES(120, working loads advance first), SET menuData+0x5d=1 so the child walks 18->Cleanup->Finish->-1; then CLEAR it (ENDING_REQUEST_SET latch) the frame mms_step leaves 18 -- before the resident-world bounce window. Semaphore: ENDING_REQUEST_SET_COUNT>0 + ENDING-REQUEST RECOVERY log SET then clear + MMS-FINISH + LOADED_STABLE + world enters, and NO bounce (world stays up). This is the 2nd stale-teardown-timing flag (bc4 was the 1st); both are our fast-switch teardown clearing a flag a later load still needs.
